topics.name: Economics Fundamentals and Production
topics.description: JAMB UTME Section I: Economic methodology, scarcity, choice, opportunity cost, and production systems.

subtopics.name: Economic Concepts and Methodology
objectives: Explain basic economic tools: tables, graphs, charts, measures of central tendency
Analyze scarcity, choice, scale of preference, opportunity cost, and economic systems (capitalist, socialist, mixed)

subtopics.name: Production and Business Units
objectives: Explain factors of production, division of labor, specialization, and production functions
Compare sole proprietorships, partnerships, joint-stock companies, and public corporations

topics.name: Microeconomics: Demand, Supply, Prices, and Markets
topics.description: JAMB UTME Section II: Demand, supply, price determination, elasticities, utility, and market structures.

subtopics.name: Demand, Supply, and Price Equilibrium
objectives: Calculate market equilibrium price and quantity algebraically and graphically
Calculate Price Elasticity of Demand (PED), Supply (PES), Income (YED), and Cross Elasticity (XED)

subtopics.name: Utility Theory and Market Structures
objectives: Apply Cardinal Utility (Law of Diminishing Marginal Utility) and Ordinal Utility (indifference curves)
Compare price and output determination in Perfect Competition, Monopoly, Monopolistic Competition, and Oligopoly

topics.name: Macroeconomics: Money, Public Finance, and International Trade
topics.description: JAMB UTME Section III & IV: National income, inflation, money, banking, public finance, population, and trade.

subtopics.name: Money, Banking, and Inflation
objectives: Explain functions of money, commercial bank credit creation, and central bank monetary policy tools
Analyze causes, effects, and fiscal/monetary solutions for inflation and deflation

subtopics.name: National Income, Public Finance, and Trade
objectives: Calculate GDP, GNP, NNP, and Per Capita Income using Output, Income, and Expenditure approaches
Analyze government budget, taxation canons, balance of payments, and international trade barriers/agreements
